envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Authority Map intake establishes delegated-authority boundaries. Per ADR-0026 + ADR-0050: dual-control + break-glass. Per RULE 0: humans always sovereign; AI cannot grant access to other AI.

items.name: Authority Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Authority Map. Maps approval chains, delegated authority profiles, proxy actions, spend thresholds, external-communication authority, connector-write gates, dual-control requirements, and what must never be assumed by any Twin or AI agent.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks who can approve what, who can act for whom, what must never be assumed. Outputs Authority Map candidates pending admin approval. AI never grants authority autonomously.
items.model_usage_notes: Per RULE 0: humans are always sovereign. Per ADR-0080 + Wave 2.1 §19-20: delegated authority profiles are explicit + revocable. AI cannot grant access to other AI (RULE 0).
required_questions: What approval chains exist (spend / hiring / external commit / legal / customer commitment)?
What delegated authority does each role hold (per Wave 2.1 §19 delegated-authority profiles)?
What proxy actions are permitted (EA-as-executive / acting-manager / interim approver)?
What spend thresholds apply at which approver level?
What external-communication authority applies (customer / press / regulator / public)?
What connector-write gates apply per ADR-0084 (risky writes require approval / dual-control)?
optional_questions: What dual-control requirements exist per ADR-0026?
Who owns each policy (acceptable use / data handling / vendor approval)?
What break-glass / emergency authority exists per ADR-0050?
What temporary delegations are common (PTO / TDY / interim)?
Who must never be assumed by any Twin (e.g., founder voice / CEO email / board member)?
admin_questions: Which approval chains must the Map represent at first activation?
Which authority surfaces are RULE-20-protected and must NOT be modifiable from assessment?
Which dual-control workflows are mandatory regardless of plan tier?
Which authority paths require ADR-0050 break-glass governance?
What 'must never be assumed' rules apply org-wide?
user_questions: Does the Map represent your approval / delegation context accurately?
Are there authority surfaces you (the user being asked) should not be the source of (legal / HR / finance)?
evidence_sources: Wave 2.1 §17-20 per-role delegated authority + approval dependencies
OOTB DelegatedAuthorityProfile + PermissionBundle templates at docs/ootb-catalog/
output_candidates: Authority Map proposal (approval chains / delegated authority / proxy actions / spend thresholds)
Dual-control requirement list per ADR-0026 (admin approval required)
Connector-write gate list per ADR-0084 (admin approval required)
governance_review_points: Admin must approve every Authority Map region
RULE 0 sovereignty is absolute: AI never grants authority to other AI
Dual-control mandatory for risky writes per ADR-0026
ADR-0050 break-glass for emergency authority only
Per-call governance approval required for any authority-bound execution
Authority Map regions must compose against Foundation governance — never override it
DMW_scope_implications: Authority Map bounds Twin write-side DMW provenance
Dual-control workflows constrain write-side DMW emission tracking
'Must never be assumed' authority surfaces excluded from any Twin DMW
Break-glass authority per ADR-0050 emits to bounded audit-only DMW scope
audit_expectations: future BREAK_GLASS_* at runtime per ADR-0050
